title: The Unthrift
content:
Here in the shade of the tree
    The hours go by
    Silent and swift,
    Lightly as birds fly.
    Then the deep clouds broaden and drift,
    Or the cloudless darkness and the worn moon.
    Waking, the dreamer knows he is old,
    And the day that he dreamed was gone
    Is gone.
meaning:
"The Unthrift" is a quintessential example of John Frederick Freeman's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
